Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the Dr. Mehta relocation context using search_emails (query: "Mehta" or "Anand Mehta" or "Canopy executive relocation"). Expected findings: Patricia Langford's original request for the executive relocation, Mina Hashimoto's escalation to ops, Fatimah's coordination plan, Dr. Mehta's equipment inventory response, and May 5 first-day-on-site target.
OE 2: Search emails for any chain-of-custody / Canopy compliance correspondence using search_emails (query: "chain of custody" or "Canopy compliance" or "Patricia Langford"). Expected finding: status of whether Patricia ever connected Fatimah with the Canopy compliance team or sent the chain-of-custody documentation template referenced in Linear ticket 375.
OE 3: Search emails for the Chicago temporary housing options using search_emails (query: "Carmen Reyes" or "UrbanNest" or "River North"). Expected findings: Carmen Reyes's reply with three premium furnished options for Dr. Mehta -- Option A: The Langham Residences in River North ($1,250/wk), Option B: The Pinnacle in Streeterville ($1,350/wk, Carmen's recommendation), Option C: The Elysian in Gold Coast ($1,450/wk). All 2BR doorman buildings near Northwestern Memorial Hospital. Fatimah forwarded the three options to Dr. Mehta on April 23 with a lock-in deadline of April 25 EOD; no Mehta selection reply exists in the inbox, so the lock-in is still pending past the deadline.
OE 4: Search emails for the Swift Relocations electronics-specialist quote using search_emails (query: "Swift Relocations" or "Greg Pallone" or "Mehta pack" or "April 30 pack"). Expected finding: NO Fatimah<->Greg Pallone correspondence about the Mehta Boston->Chicago move exists in either the SENT or INBOX folders. The agent should discover that the Swift outreach for the April 30 pack date with the electronics-specialist crew (needed for the Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated diagnostic monitors) has not yet been initiated and flag it as the most urgent gap -- this means there is no quote because there is no request, and the request must go out today to preserve the April 30 pack date.
OE 4b: Send the missing Swift Relocations quote-request email to Greg Pallone using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com"], cc: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: "URGENT -- Quote Request -- Boston->Chicago Executive Pack -- Dr. Anand Mehta -- April 30 pack with electronics-specialist crew", content: full request covering (a) Boston pickup target April 30 at latest, Chicago delivery target May 2-3, must-arrive May 5 for first day at Canopy HQ; (b) full white-glove pack and load -- partner Priya Mehta is 7 months pregnant and must do zero physical effort; (c) electronics-specialist crew required for two Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated diagnostic monitors (~$11,800 each, serial numbers RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, anti-static crating, calibration certificates must travel physically with units); (d) SentrySafe DH-109E secured filing cabinet (~350 lbs, locked dual electronic + key, chain-of-custody log required at Boston origin and Chicago destination, no warehouse interim, direct transport only); (e) Boston origin is a 3rd-floor walkup with a narrow stairwell -- no elevator at origin; (f) request crew availability confirmation and full quote by EOD tomorrow at the latest). This OE fires because OE 4 deterministically discovers that no prior Fatimah->Greg Mehta outreach exists in the inbox, making the "chase him today if I never actually sent it" instruction mandatory rather than conditional.
OE 5: Search Slack for Mehta-related discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "Canopy executive" or "River North"). Expected findings: any internal #operations or #customer-engagement discussion of the Dr. Mehta workstreams, capacity flags from Chloe, or Mina Hashimoto's CRM updates.
OE 6: List Linear issues for the Canopy project using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta", project: "proj_canopy"). Expected findings: ticket 343 "Temporary Housing -- Dr. Anand Mehta -- Chicago River North" (in_progress, P1, due 2026-05-01), ticket 375 "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody -- Dr. Mehta -- Medical Research Equipment" (in_progress, P1, due 2026-04-30), ticket 396 "Household Move -- Dr. Anand Mehta -- Boston to Chicago" (in_progress, P1, due 2026-05-03). All are assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi.
OE 7: Get the full Temporary Housing ticket using linear_get_issue (id: "linear_issue_bd446671ceed"). Expected finding: full description with action items including contacting Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest, presenting top 3 options to Dr. Mehta, locking in by April 23 EOD, key handoff May 2-3.
OE 8: Get the full Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody ticket using linear_get_issue (id: "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a"). Expected finding: equipment inventory (two Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors ~$12,000 each, SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet ~350 lbs containing patient research data binders), chain-of-custody requirements, signed handoff log at Boston origin and Chicago destination, no warehouse interim, calibration certificates must travel with monitors, action item to obtain template from Patricia Langford / Canopy compliance.
OE 9: Get the full Household Move ticket using linear_get_issue (id: "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb"). Expected finding: Swift Relocations as preferred vendor, target April 30 pack, May 2-3 delivery, May 5 must-arrive, partner Priya Mehta 7 months pregnant, dependencies on temp housing and equipment packing workstreams.
OE 10: List comments on ticket 343 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_bd446671ceed") to surface any updates on Carmen Reyes responses or housing lock-in status.
OE 11: List comments on ticket 375 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a") to surface any updates on the chain-of-custody template or equipment inventory confirmation.
OE 12: List comments on ticket 396 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb") to surface any updates on the Swift quote or Boston address confirmation.
OE 13: List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases to discover the MoveOps Operations base. Expected finding: appMoveOpsOps001 -- MoveOps Operations.
OE 14: List tables in the Operations base using airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001"). Expected finding: tblRelocations01 (Relocations), tblStipends00001 (Stipend Transactions), tblClientAccts01 (Client Accounts).
OE 15: Search the Relocations table for any existing Dr. Mehta record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Anand Mehta"). Expected finding: NO existing record for Dr. Anand Mehta -- confirming the gap referenced in the prompt.
OE 16: Look up Patricia Langford's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford"). Expected finding: patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io (Canopy Health Director of HR).
OE 17: Look up Chloe Vance's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe Vance"). Expected finding: chloe.vance@moveops.com (Operations Manager).
OE 18: Check Fatimah's existing calendar events for April 28 and April 29, 2026 using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-28T00:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-29T23:59:59") to find an open 30-minute slot before the April 30 Boston pack date.
OE 19: Create the missing Airtable Relocations record for Dr. Anand Mehta using airtable_create_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", fields: { Name: "Dr. Anand Mehta", Company: "Canopy Health", Status: "In Progress", Origin: "Boston, MA", Destination: "Chicago, IL", Move Start Date: "2026-04-30", Move End Date: "2026-05-03", Assigned Coordinator: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", Special Requirements: "Executive / VIP white-glove relocation. First C-suite placement for Canopy Health. Two Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated diagnostic monitors (~$12,000 each), SentrySafe DH-109E secured filing cabinet (~350 lbs) with patient research data -- chain-of-custody required at Boston origin and Chicago destination, no warehouse interim. Vendor: Swift Relocations electronics-specialist crew. Temp housing in River North/Streeterville via UrbanNest (Carmen Reyes), 3-4 weeks. Partner Priya Mehta 7 months pregnant -- full white-glove, zero physical effort. Must arrive by May 5 for first day at Canopy HQ." }).
OE 20: For each open workstream still at risk against the May 5 deadline, add an urgency comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_bd446671ceed" or "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a" or "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb", body: "Urgent -- Chloe wants a status before her ops review. Less than 10 days to the May 5 first-day-at-Canopy hard deadline. Flagging this workstream as still at risk: <specific blocker discovered during investigation>.").
OE 21: Conditional fallback -- only if the investigation surfaces a blocker that does not map to an existing ticket. Note: tickets 343 (housing), 375 (chain-of-custody), and 396 (household move) already cover all three Mehta workstreams, so OE 20 (comment on existing ticket) is the expected path for the known gaps. OE 21 only fires if a genuinely new blocker surfaces. In that case, create a new tracking issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", title: "Dr. Mehta relocation -- <blocker>", description: "<details of the gap and what is needed before May 5>", assignee: "moveops_fatimah_alrashidi", project: "proj_canopy", priority: 1, dueDate: "2026-04-30").
OE 22: Send the status email to Chloe using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"], subject: "Dr. Mehta relocation -- workstream status", content: full status covering (a) temporary housing -- Carmen Reyes / UrbanNest options status and lock-in status, (b) chain-of-custody packing -- Eizo monitors and SentrySafe cabinet, whether Patricia Langford / Canopy compliance template was received, (c) household move -- Swift / Greg Pallone April 30 pack-date quote status, (d) blockers and what is needed before May 5).
OE 23: Post a status heads-up in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: "Heads-up on the Dr. Mehta executive relocation (first C-suite for Canopy Health, May 5 hard deadline): housing -- <status>; chain-of-custody packing -- <status>; household move -- <status>. Open blockers: <list>. Coordinators on call should be aware in case anything escalates.").
OE 24: Schedule a 30-minute prep call with Patricia Langford on Fatimah's calendar for April 28 or April 29 using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Dr. Mehta relocation -- chain-of-custody handoff prep with Patricia Langford", start_datetime: "2026-04-28T15:00:00" or another open 30-minute slot on April 28 or April 29 found in OE 18, end_datetime: corresponding +30 minutes, attendees: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io", "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com"], description: "Walk Patricia through the chain-of-custody handoff process for the Eizo RadiForce monitors and SentrySafe filing cabinet ahead of the April 30 Boston pack date.").

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the Dr. Mehta relocation context using search_emails (query: "Mehta" or "Anand Mehta" or "Canopy executive relocation"). Expected findings: Patricia Langford's original request for the executive relocation, Mina Hashimoto's escalation to ops, Fatimah's coordination plan, Dr. Mehta's equipment inventory response, and May 5 first-day-on-site target.
OE 2: Search emails for any chain-of-custody / Canopy compliance correspondence using search_emails (query: "chain of custody" or "Canopy compliance" or "Patricia Langford"). Expected finding: status of whether Patricia ever connected Fatimah with the Canopy compliance team or sent the chain-of-custody documentation template referenced in Linear ticket 375.
OE 3: Search emails for the Chicago temporary housing options using search_emails (query: "Carmen Reyes" or "UrbanNest" or "River North"). Expected findings: Carmen Reyes's reply with three premium furnished options for Dr. Mehta -- Option A: The Langham Residences in River North ($1,250/wk), Option B: The Pinnacle in Streeterville ($1,350/wk, Carmen's recommendation), Option C: The Elysian in Gold Coast ($1,450/wk). All 2BR doorman buildings near Northwestern Memorial Hospital. Fatimah forwarded the three options to Dr. Mehta on April 23 with a lock-in deadline of April 25 EOD; no Mehta selection reply exists in the inbox, so the lock-in is still pending past the deadline.
OE 4: Search emails for the Swift Relocations electronics-specialist quote using search_emails (query: "Swift Relocations" or "Greg Pallone" or "Mehta pack" or "April 30 pack"). Expected finding: NO Fatimah<->Greg Pallone correspondence about the Mehta Boston->Chicago move exists in either the SENT or INBOX folders. The agent should discover that the Swift outreach for the April 30 pack date with the electronics-specialist crew (needed for the Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated diagnostic monitors) has not yet been initiated and flag it as the most urgent gap -- this means there is no quote because there is no request, and the request must go out today to preserve the April 30 pack date.
OE 4b: Send the missing Swift Relocations quote-request email to Greg Pallone using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com"], cc: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com"], subject: "URGENT -- Quote Request -- Boston->Chicago Executive Pack -- Dr. Anand Mehta -- April 30 pack with electronics-specialist crew", content: full request covering (a) Boston pickup target April 30 at latest, Chicago delivery target May 2-3, must-arrive May 5 for first day at Canopy HQ; (b) full white-glove pack and load -- partner Priya Mehta is 7 months pregnant and must do zero physical effort; (c) electronics-specialist crew required for two Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated diagnostic monitors (~$11,800 each, serial numbers RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, anti-static crating, calibration certificates must travel physically with units); (d) SentrySafe DH-109E secured filing cabinet (~350 lbs, locked dual electronic + key, chain-of-custody log required at Boston origin and Chicago destination, no warehouse interim, direct transport only); (e) Boston origin is a 3rd-floor walkup with a narrow stairwell -- no elevator at origin; (f) request crew availability confirmation and full quote by EOD tomorrow at the latest). This OE fires because OE 4 deterministically discovers that no prior Fatimah->Greg Mehta outreach exists in the inbox, making the "chase him today if I never actually sent it" instruction mandatory rather than conditional.
OE 5: Search Slack for Mehta-related discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mehta" or "Canopy executive" or "River North"). Expected findings: any internal #operations or #customer-engagement discussion of the Dr. Mehta workstreams, capacity flags from Chloe, or Mina Hashimoto's CRM updates.
OE 6: List Linear issues for the Canopy project using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta", project: "proj_canopy"). Expected findings: ticket 343 "Temporary Housing -- Dr. Anand Mehta -- Chicago River North" (in_progress, P1, due 2026-05-01), ticket 375 "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody -- Dr. Mehta -- Medical Research Equipment" (in_progress, P1, due 2026-04-30), ticket 396 "Household Move -- Dr. Anand Mehta -- Boston to Chicago" (in_progress, P1, due 2026-05-03). All are assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi.
OE 7: Get the full Temporary Housing ticket using linear_get_issue (id: "linear_issue_bd446671ceed"). Expected finding: full description with action items including contacting Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest, presenting top 3 options to Dr. Mehta, locking in by April 23 EOD, key handoff May 2-3.
OE 8: Get the full Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody ticket using linear_get_issue (id: "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a"). OE 9: Get the full Household Move ticket using linear_get_issue (id: "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb"). OE 12: List comments on ticket 396 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb") to surface any updates on the Swift quote or Boston address confirmation.

OE 14: List tables in the Operations base using airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001"). Expected finding: tblRelocations01 (Relocations), tblStipends00001 (Stipend Transactions), tblClientAccts01 (Client Accounts).

OE 19: Create the missing Airtable Relocations record for Dr. Anand Mehta using airtable_create_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", fields: { Name: "Dr. Anand Mehta", Company: "Canopy Health", Status: "In Progress", Origin: "Boston, MA", Destination: "Chicago, IL", Move Start Date: "2026-04-30", Move End Date: "2026-05-03", Assigned Coordinator: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", Special Requirements: "Executive / VIP white-glove relocation. OE 20: For each open workstream still at risk against the May 5 deadline, add an urgency comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_bd446671ceed" or "linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a" or "linear_issue_4902f5c68acb", body: "Urgent -- Chloe wants a status before her ops review. Less than 10 days to the May 5 first-day-at-Canopy hard deadline. Flagging this workstream as still at risk: <specific blocker discovered during investigation>.").
OE 21: Conditional fallback -- only if the investigation surfaces a blocker that does not map to an existing ticket. Note: tickets 343 (housing), 375 (chain-of-custody), and 396 (household move) already cover all three Mehta workstreams, so OE 20 (comment on existing ticket) is the expected path for the known gaps. OE 21 only fires if a genuinely new blocker surfaces. In that case, create a new tracking issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", title: "Dr. Mehta relocation -- <blocker>", description: "<details of the gap and what is needed before May 5>", assignee: "moveops_fatimah_alrashidi", project: "proj_canopy", priority: 1, dueDate: "2026-04-30").
OE 22: Send the status email to Chloe using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"], subject: "Dr. Mehta relocation -- workstream status", content: full status covering (a) temporary housing -- Carmen Reyes / UrbanNest options status and lock-in status, (b) chain-of-custody packing -- Eizo monitors and SentrySafe cabinet, whether Patricia Langford / Canopy compliance template was received, (c) household move -- Swift / Greg Pallone April 30 pack-date quote status, (d) blockers and what is needed before May 5).
OE 23: Post a status heads-up in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: "Heads-up on the Dr. Mehta executive relocation (first C-suite for Canopy Health, May 5 hard deadline): housing -- <status>; chain-of-custody packing -- <status>; household move -- <status>. Open blockers: <list>. Coordinators on call should be aware in case anything escalates.").
OE 24: Schedule a 30-minute prep call with Patricia Langford on Fatimah's calendar for April 28 or April 29 using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Dr. Mehta relocation -- chain-of-custody handoff prep with Patricia Langford", start_datetime: "2026-04-28T15:00:00" or another open 30-minute slot on April 28 or April 29 found in OE 18, end_datetime: corresponding +30 minutes, attendees: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io", "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com"], description: "Walk Patricia through the chain-of-custody handoff process for the Eizo RadiForce monitors and SentrySafe filing cabinet ahead of the April 30 Boston pack date.").

19 At least one Linear comment body (in linear_create_comment body parameter) on a Dr. Mehta workstream ticket references the May 5, 2026 Canopy Health start deadline and flags the workstream as urgent or at-risk (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1All three Linear comments reference the May 5 deadline and flag urgency. For example, the comment on linear_issue_bd446671ceed states 'This is the first C-suite placement for our longest-standing client. The May 5 start date is firm and non-negotiable.' The comment on linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a says 'This is now a critical blocker' and the comment on linear_issue_4902f5c68acb says 'Timeline risk: April 30 pack date is only 4 days out'.
✓Run #2The housing ticket comment states 'Dr. Mehta starts at Canopy on May 5 — 9 days from now' and flags the workstream as 'URGENT.' The chain-of-custody comment mentions the April 30 pack date (4 days away). All three comments contain urgency language.
